MOTION FOR LEAVE TO First Amended Answer
Off Calendar Nov-12-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT DAVE'S BUILDING MAINTENANCE, INC.'s MOTION FOR LEAVE TO First Amended Answer.

Defendant Dave's Building Maintenance, Inc.'s unopposed Motion for Leave to File First Amended Answer is GRANTED.

Civil Procedure section 473(a)(1): "The court may, in furtherance of justice, and on any terms as may be proper, allow a party to amend any pleading or proceeding by adding or striking out the name of any party, or by correcting a mistake in the name of a party, or a mistake in any other respect; and may, upon like terms, enlarge the time for answer or demurrer. The court may likewise, in its discretion, after notice to the adverse party, allow, upon any terms as may be just, an amendment to any pleading or proceeding in other particulars; and may upon like terms allow an answer to be made after the time limited by this code." Defendant moves, as well, under Code of Civil Procedure section 576, which states that "[a]ny judge, at any time before or after commencement of trial, in the furtherance of justice, and upon such terms as may be proper, may allow the amendment of any pleading or pretrial conference order." Because judicial policy favors resolution of all disputed matters between the parties in the same lawsuit, the court's discretion will usually be exercised liberally to permit amendment of the pleadings. (Edmon & Karnow, CALIFORNIA PRACTICE GUIDE: CIVIL PROCEDURE BEFORE TRIAL 6:638 (Rutter 2023); see also Nestle v. Santa Monica (1972) 6 Cal.3d 920, 939.) Indeed, "it is an abuse of discretion to deny leave to amend where the opposing party was not misled or prejudiced by the amendment." (Kittredge Sports Co. v. Superior Court (1989) 213 Cal.App.3d 1045, 1048.)

Good cause appearing, the court grants leave to Moving Defendant to file an amended answer substantially identical to Exhibit A of the Eberts Declaration. Defendant must file the amended answer within 10 days of this order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Nov-06-2025 Continued To Jun-29-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Of David Garibaldi
Off Calendar Oct-20-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances via Zoom Conference:
Oliver Vallejo, Esq., of THE VALLEJO LAW OFFICE, Tel: (415) 722-2173 for plaintiffs.
Adam Eberts, Esq., of LAW OFFICES OF JILL A. WOOD, Tel: 714-571-0407 for defendant.

Hearing held. A timely Notice of Contest was received from Plaintiff's counsel regarding sanction. The plaintiff counsel stated that they fulfilled their responsibilities that the sanctions would not be appropriate. The matter argued/discussed. The Court having heard oral argument simultaneously the 2 motions and having reviewed the pleadings, the Court adopts its tentative ruling as modified:

2 - Plaintiffs Elba Vazquez and Francisco Gonzalez's Motion to Compel Deposition of David Garibaldi is DENIED.

There is no demonstration by either party of meet and confer efforts for the issues presented in this motion prior to filing this motion. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 2016.040.) In looking at the declarations of Oliver Vallejo and Adam Eberts and their attached exhibits, the parties had a deposition noticed for October 10, 2025. Defendant produced David Garibaldi for deposition on October 10, 2025. (Vallejo Reply Decl. 2.) In totality, there was no need for this motion to be filed at all as Plaintiffs failed to make any efforts to resolve this dispute on their own before filing a motion to compel.

Sanctions are stricken.

Parties are advised to meet and confer in good faith prior to filing future discovery motions.

Order is signed.

Judge: Samuel K. Feng; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/SKF)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Response To Requests For Production, Set One; Declaration Of Oliver Vallejo
Off Calendar Oct-20-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances via Zoom Conference:
Oliver Vallejo, Esq., of THE VALLEJO LAW OFFICE, Tel: (415) 722-2173 for plaintiffs.
Adam Eberts, Esq., of LAW OFFICES OF JILL A. WOOD, Tel: 714-571-0407 for defendant.

Hearing held. A timely Notice of Contest was received from Plaintiff's counsel regarding sanction. The plaintiff counsel stated that they fulfilled their responsibilities that the sanctions would not be appropriate. The matter argued/discussed. The Court having heard oral argument simultaneously the 2 motions and having reviewed the pleadings, the Court adopts its tentative ruling as modified:

1 - Plaintiffs Elba Vazquez and Francisco Gonzalez's Motion to Compel Responses to Requests for Production is DENIED. Plaintiffs withdrew their motion in their reply from October 2, 2025.

Plaintiff failed to demonstrate adequate meet and confer for the issues presented in this motion. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 2016.040.) If the parties had met and conferred prior to filing this motion, Plaintiffs may have been alerted to their mistake and saved both parties and the court's resources.

Sanctions are stricken.

Parties are advised to meet and confer in good faith prior to filing future discovery motions.

Order is signed.

Judge: Samuel K. Feng; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/SKF)
